title: Getting Started
code: obj <- scdown(scdown_example()) obj #> <scdown_obj> #>   cells: 48  #>   genes: 15  #>   samples: 4  #>   annotations: 6  #>   value type: counts

title: Getting Started
code: map_res <- plot_map(obj) marker_res <- explore_markers(obj, top_n = 3) gene_res <- plot_gene(obj, genes = c("CD3D", "NKG7", "LYZ")) sig_res <- plot_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "b_cell"))  kable(head(map_res$tables$embedding_cells, 6)) kable(marker_res$tables$top_marker_per_celltype) kable(gene_res$tables$gene_average_by_celltype) kable(sig_res$tables$signature_score_by_celltype)

title: Getting Started
code: comp_test <- test_composition(obj) sig_test <- test_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "b_cell"))  kable(comp_test$tables$composition_test_table) kable(sig_test$tables$signature_test_table)

title: Getting Started
code: obj_custom <- scdown(   scdown_example(),   signatures = list(my_state = c("CD3D", "IL7R")),   marker_panels = list(my_t_panel = c("CD3D", "TRAC")) )  kable(plot_signature(obj_custom, signatures = "my_state")$tables$signature_score_by_celltype)

title: Getting Started
code: report_path <- build_scdown_report(obj) report_path #> [1] "C:\\Users\\daiki\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\RtmpOcU3lh\\scdown-report-5c3428274113/scdown_report.html"

title: Recommended Pipeline
text: Use scdown standard PBMC preprocessing annotation. usually enough goal : checking whether annotation looks sensible comparing known immune signatures sharing compact report collaborators

title: Recommended Pipeline
text: Tumor-infiltrating immune datasets strong fit scdown review layer, especially : markers user-selected genes composition immune signatures exploratory communication
code: kable(recommended_pipeline("tumor_immune"))

title: Recommended Pipeline
text: donor-aware patient-vs-patient comparisons, scdown still useful, mainly layer decides promoted formal models. scenario usual handoff : scdown review, prioritization, compact reports dreamlet muscat differential state miloR abundance-style shifts CellChat another specialist framework communication primary claim
code: kable(recommended_pipeline("patient_comparison"))

title: Recommended Pipeline
text: question : “annotated dataset look like?” “cell types signatures stand ?” “Can quickly share report?” scdown good home. question : “final model publish?” “need covariates, random effects, formal abundance modeling?” scdown handoff point, final engine.

title: scdown
text: scdown simple downstream toolkit annotated single-cell RNA-seq data. best used QC, normalization, embedding, clustering, cell annotation. words, scdown downstream hub, full upstream single-cell framework.

title: scdown
text: make one downstream analysis object run fast exploratory summaries run sample-aware tests need export compact HTML report review collaboration

title: scdown
text: droplet QC ambient RNA correction normalization, HVG selection, integration, clustering, trajectory fitting publication-grade differential state primary specialist method parts better handled upstream specialist tools Seurat, scater, scuttle, scran, SingleR, muscat, dreamlet, miloR, CellChat. Internally, scdown keeps data sparse-first matrix form, longer needs expand everything huge long table analysis.

what: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
title: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
text: scdown compact downstream toolkit annotated single-cell RNA-seq data. standardizes common input types, keeps internal representation sparse-first, separates lightweight exploration sample-aware testing, acts downstream hub annotation: maps, markers, genes interest, composition, annotation checks, signatures, communication, compact reports, scope tables, handoff exports, guidance hand specialist tools.

title: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
text: Maintainer: Daiki User daik54015@gmail.com

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
code: scdown(   x,   annotation_col = "cell_type",   sample_col = "sample",   group_col = "group",   batch_col = NULL,   reduced_dim = "UMAP",   embedding = NULL,   assay_name = NULL,   expression_layer = NULL,   signatures = NULL,   marker_panels = NULL,   lineage_mapping = NULL,   lr_pairs = NULL )

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
text: x long-format expression table, list expr, optional cells, optional embedding, SingleCellExperiment / Seurat object. annotation_col Cell annotation column name. sample_col Sample column name. group_col Optional group column name. batch_col Optional batch column name. reduced_dim Name reduced dimension use. embedding Optional embedding data frame cell, UMAP1, UMAP2. assay_name Optional assay name SingleCellExperiment Seurat. expression_layer Optional Seurat layer/slot name. signatures Optional named list custom signatures. marker_panels Optional named list custom marker panels. lineage_mapping Optional data frame pattern lineage. lr_pairs Optional data frame ligand, receptor, pathway.

title: scdown 0.4.1
text: added release workflow can publish GitHub releases pushed version tags expanded CI single Linux check multi-OS matrix extra devel check Linux split strengthened tests around sparse inputs, failure modes, report output, handoff schemas added repository-facing metadata files CITATION.cff issue templates improve public package trust signals clarified HTML report built-tests screening-oriented hand specialist methods primary claims

title: scdown 0.4.0
text: added scope_table() make intended boundary scdown explicit added export_for_handoff() prepare objects muscat, dreamlet, miloR, CellChat workflows expanded build_scdown_report() scope guidance plus marker communication test sections strengthened README, pkgdown, recommended pipeline vignette around exploratory-vs-specialist handoff

title: scdown 0.3.0
text: clarified scdown annotation-downstream hub rather full upstream workflow added recommended pipeline helper handoff vignette PBMC, tumor immune, patient comparison use cases updated README pkgdown site explain scdown fits specialist tools use

title: scdown 0.2.0
text: switched sparse-first internal matrix representation added custom signatures, marker panels, lineage mapping, ligand-receptor pairs split exploratory functions sample-aware testing functions added CI workflows build hygiene files

title: scdown 0.1.0
